metadata.source: IP (1).pdf - EMG Signal Interpretation Using LLMs

rules.title: Baseline Noise
rules.rule: Resting baseline noise RMS below 0.005 mV (5 uV) is good. Above 0.010 mV = POOR QUALITY (bad skin prep or electrode contact).

rules.title: SNR Threshold
rules.rule: >15 dB reliable. 10-15 dB MARGINAL. <10 dB UNRELIABLE.

rules.title: Powerline Contamination
rules.rule: PowerlineRatio >0.10 = significant PLI. Recommend notch/adaptive cancellation; check grounding.

rules.title: Clipping/Saturation
rules.rule: ClippingRatio >0.01 or >0.5% samples at extreme percentiles = amplifier saturation. Reduce gain.

rules.title: Spectral Shape
rules.rule: Broadband 10-500 Hz expected, peaking 50-150 Hz. Spectral entropy <3.0 bits = single-frequency artifact.

rules.title: Cross-talk
rules.rule: Opposite-side sensors correlated >0.8 during unilateral gesture = crosstalk. Review electrode spacing.

rules.title: Motion Artifact
rules.rule: DC offset shift >0.5 mV between segments + low-freq dominance (<5 Hz) = motion artifact. Exclude segments.

rules.title: Dropout/Flatline
rules.rule: DropoutPct >0.5% or flatline >200 ms = possible disconnection. Mark segment unusable.

rules.title: LF Artifact
rules.rule: LF_artifact_ratio (1-10 Hz / 20-450 Hz) >0.30 = motion/cable artifact. Check fixation; consider high-pass 20 Hz.

rules.title: Anomalous Distribution
rules.rule: Kurtosis >10 = spike artifacts. Unusually high ZC/SSC relative to RMS = noise-dominated signal.

rules.title: Drift Rate
rules.rule: DriftSlope >0.001 mV/s = baseline wander. Mild: 0.001-0.005. Moderate: 0.005-0.01. Severe: >0.01 mV/s.

rules.title: DC Offset Trend
rules.rule: Monotonic change >0.05 mV over 5+ consecutive segments = systematic drift.

rules.title: Noise Floor Drift
rules.rule: BaselineNoiseRMS increase >50% from first to last 5 segments = interface degradation.

rules.title: Impedance Drift
rules.rule: Simultaneous noise increase + SNR decrease = rising impedance. Common after 15-20 min.

rules.title: Sudden Shift
rules.rule: DCOffset change >0.1 mV between consecutive segments = electrode displacement. Flag for exclusion.

rules.title: Perspiration
rules.rule: Gradual noise decrease in first 5-10 min is normal (skin hydration). Concern if it then suddenly increases.

rules.title: Warm-Up Stabilization
rules.rule: Electrode-skin impedance changes during first 10-15 min. Use minute 15+ as stable baseline for trend analysis.

rules.title: Amplitude-Frequency Dissociation
rules.rule: RMS change >20% but MDF change <5% = sweat/impedance drift, not fatigue. Fatigue requires concurrent MDF decline.

rules.title: Step Change Detection
rules.rule: CUSUM or change-point on RMS/activation map. Step >3xMAD = electrode displacement or cable tug. Annotate timing.

rules.title: Spatial Reconfiguration
rules.rule: Activation map cosine similarity <0.7 for 3+ segments = spatial pattern change. May indicate electrode shift or motor compensation.

rules.title: MDF Decline (Gold Standard)
rules.rule: 10-20% decline from baseline = moderate fatigue. >20% = severe.

rules.title: MNF Parallel
rules.rule: MNF should decline with MDF. MNF-only decline may be noise/artifact.

rules.title: RMS Increase
rules.rule: Sustained contraction RMS increases 25-50% from motor unit recruitment. RMS up + MDF down = strong fatigue.

rules.title: L/H Ratio
rules.rule: Increase >30% from baseline = spectral compression consistent with fatigue.

rules.title: Entropy Decrease
rules.rule: SampleEntropy decline >15% = fatigue-related motor unit synchronization.

rules.title: ZC Decrease
rules.rule: >10% decrease supports fatigue (slowed conduction velocity).

rules.title: Onset Detection
rules.rule: Onset = 3+ consecutive MDF-declining segments. Report time in seconds.

rules.title: Fatigue Index
rules.rule: FI = (MDF_baseline - MDF_final) / MDF_baseline x 100%. Mild: 5-10%. Moderate: 10-20%. Severe: >20%.

rules.title: Multi-sensor Consistency
rules.rule: Multiple sensors fatiguing simultaneously strengthens finding.

rules.title: Confound Check
rules.rule: If SNR drops concurrently, electrode degradation is more likely than fatigue.

rules.title: MDF Slope Rate
rules.rule: Track Hz/min. Sustained -0.1 to -0.5 Hz/min = fatigue. Outside this range = possible artifact.

rules.title: Spatial Compensation
rules.rule: If concentration index decreases while total RMS is maintained, load is redistributing. Dominant sensor may change mid-session.

rules.title: Prerequisite Gate
rules.rule: Before concluding fatigue, confirm: (a) quality GOOD/MARGINAL, (b) drift None/Mild, (c) D8 rule excludes sweat-only amplitude change.

rules.title: Activation Level
rules.rule: RMS relative to session mean. Low: <30%. Moderate: 30-70%. High: 70-100%. Very High: >100%.

rules.title: Gesture Patterns
rules.rule: Flexion = S1-S4 (palmar). Extension = S5-S8. Deviations = placement error or compensation.

rules.title: Co-contraction
rules.rule: Agonist + antagonist RMS both >50% peak simultaneously. Normal for power grip; abnormal for fine motor.

rules.title: Consistency
rules.rule: CV of RMS. <20% consistent. 20-40% moderate. >40% difficulty maintaining gesture.

rules.title: Dominant Sensor
rules.rule: Highest average RMS. Report dominant-to-weakest ratio.

rules.title: Temporal Profile
rules.rule: Sustained, Ramp-up, Ramp-down, Intermittent, or Compensatory shift.

rules.title: Force Estimation
rules.rule: RMS has linear-to-quadratic relationship with force. Compare across patients.

rules.title: Motor Unit Recruitment
rules.rule: Higher WAMP/ZC = fast-twitch recruitment (increasing effort or fatigue).
rules.source: Phinyomark et al. 2012, Nazmi et al. 2016

rules.title: Onset Detection
rules.rule: Threshold = baseline mean + k*SD (k in [1,3]) with minimum duration 20-100 ms. Per Hodges & Bui 1996.

rules.title: Envelope Smoothing
rules.rule: Rectify + low-pass. Use <=25 ms for timing accuracy; up to 250 ms for amplitude trends.

rules.title: False Onset Guard
rules.rule: Require consecutive windows above threshold. Single-window crossings = noise spikes.

rules.title: Duty Cycle
rules.rule: % time above activation threshold per sensor. Sustained vs intermittent classification.

rules.title: Normalization
rules.rule: Report as %MVC or %baseline. Without normalization, cross-session/subject comparisons are unreliable.

rules.title: EMG Range
rules.rule: Meaningful 10-500 Hz. <10 Hz = motion artifact. >500 Hz = noise.

rules.title: Motor Units
rules.rule: 100-1000 fibers each. Henneman size principle. Fast-twitch = higher freq EMG.

rules.title: Conduction Velocity
rules.rule: Normal 3-6 m/s. Fatigue reduces it, shifting spectrum lower.

rules.title: Typical Amplitude
rules.rule: 0-6 mV p-p. Forearm moderate grip: 0.1-1.0 mV RMS. <0.01 mV = not over muscle.

rules.title: Session Phases
rules.rule: 0-5 min warmup, 5-10 stable, 10-15 early fatigue, 15-20 pronounced. Impedance stabilizes ~10-15 min.

rules.title: Inter-Subject Variability
rules.rule: 3-5x amplitude variation. Within-subject trends far more reliable.

rules.title: Amplitude Caution
rules.rule: sEMG amplitude cannot infer absolute force, compare between muscles, or determine specific activation in isolation. Valid for relative changes within the same muscle/session/electrode setup.
